core_model.structure_summary: A scalar field on a one-dimensional continuum obeys a second-order-in-time wave equation with a linear damping term, a sinusoidal nonlinear restoring term of the field itself, external forcing, and an additive noise source, driven through the boundary.
core_model.canonical_form: \partial_t^2 u - \partial_x^2 u + b\,\partial_t u + \sin u = F(x,t) + \xi(x,t), \qquad \partial_x u(0,t) = f(t),\; \partial_x u(L,t) = 0

variables.meaning: scalar field on a one-dimensional continuum
one continuous spatial coordinate with a wave (d'Alembertian) operator
amplitude and frequency of the sinusoidal boundary/external forcing

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A scalar field on a one-dimensional continuum satisfies the undamped, unforced nonlinear wave equation whose restoring term is the sine of the field, supporting localized oscillatory solutions.

notes: Core model is the damped, ac-driven, thermally noisy sine-Gordon PDE (the perturbed equation is the one the numerical results are about). Stripping the dressing — remove additive noise, damping, and external/boundary forcing — leaves the bare integrable sine-Gordon wave equation u_xx - u_tt = sin(u), which is the conservative skeleton whose breather solutions the study concerns. A discrete-chain (lattice) form also appears as the numerical/cited reduction, but the continuous sine-Gordon PDE is stated as the central model.
